text:
It's a beautiful spring morning, birds are singing, the sunlight dances through the leaves, and you're sitting on your porch reading a book about obscure flowers. Suddenly, your friend calls you over because they've discovered something incredible on their hike. They've found a rare, small bird, an Ember Warbler, that sings like no other.

You meet outside and quickly realize just how tiny this bird is. It could easily perch on the tip of your pen. You decide to take it home, eager to learn more about its song and possibly share its beauty.

Weeks pass, and you find a small corner with some leaves that could serve as a cozy nest. You place the tiny bird inside, watching it flutter nervously. Over the weeks, you become its favorite person. It imitates your voice, calls your name, and even dances a little jig when you come near.
